human_questions: What is the critical user outcome?
Which component is the main service path?
Are restart failures expected or harmful?
Which metrics are zero-is-good or zero-is-bad?
Which logs indicate user impact rather than diagnostic noise?
Which discovered component roles are correct?
Which metric semantics should become explicit profile rules?

profile_to_review_links.question: What is the critical user outcome?
profile_to_review_links.reason: Targets blocked on user impact cannot be promoted until the profile question is answered with operational outcome evidence.

profile_to_review_links.question: Which metrics are zero-is-good or zero-is-bad?
profile_to_review_links.reason: Zero-count metrics can mean healthy idle, disabled routing, or broken processing; linked review targets keep that ambiguity human-gated.

profile_to_review_links.question: Which logs indicate user impact rather than diagnostic noise?
profile_to_review_links.reason: Targets blocked on user impact cannot be promoted until the profile question is answered with operational outcome evidence.

profile_to_review_links.question: Confirm critical user outcomes.
profile_to_review_links.reason: Targets blocked on user impact cannot be promoted until the profile question is answered with operational outcome evidence.

required_human_decisions: Approve or edit component roles before using this as an explicit profile.
Confirm read-only collector mappings before collection.

profile_gate.summary: Profile context was generated or approved from sanitized discovery; it constrains routing and questions, but runtime claims still require Evidence Item IDs and human-approved user outcomes.

public_positioning.note: Monitoring-plane validation: 50,000 rows compress to 21 repeated Evidence Items with 63,056 counted occurrences and 100% chunk coverage; its primary is an observability gap, not a causal incident claim.
public_positioning.title: Observation gap: stream_v3 monitoring 50k, repeated-pattern coverage

public_verification.window_selection_note: The public stream_v3 arena-server monitoring review was refreshed from a locally staged multi-week sanitized BigQuery corpus. The accepted public run uses the latest 50,000 double-sanitized rows, covering 24.9 hours and satisfying the minimum 24-hour analysis policy while keeping row-level data out of the public repo.

review_summary.target_promotion_policy: Incident gate signal is a graph-level support signal. Each review target still has its own promotion state, and promotion remains human-gated until impact and operational outcome evidence are attached to that target.

sanitized_corpus.public_note: The full row-level sanitized corpus was not committed; the public repo carries this manifest, the SHA-fixed review payload, and the public URL for reviewer inspection.

source_boundary.model_projection_policy: Single-prompt metadata records the bounded Evidence Bundle projection. Multi-provider synthesis uses chunked full-corpus Evidence Item coverage; row-level raw logs stay out of provider prompts.

title: stream_v3 arena-server monitoring 50k real API source-aware review

token_compression.interpretation: Single-prompt projection coverage is occurrence-weighted, not raw-row coverage: the full sanitized stream_v3_monitoring bundle keeps 50,000 rows and 21 grouped Evidence Items, while the bounded projection tracks 21 high-signal Evidence Items representing 63,056 repeated occurrences. Multi-provider synthesis then covered 21/21 grouped Evidence Items through chunked provider calls across up to 18 chunk(s), so low-frequency Evidence Items are analyzed instead of being dropped by frequency.
token_compression.policy: Single-prompt metadata records the bounded Evidence Bundle projection. Multi-provider synthesis uses chunked full-corpus Evidence Item coverage; row-level raw logs stay out of provider prompts.
